user.title: Morning Brief — new instance
user.description: Choose the type of brief you want to create. You can run multiple instances (one morning, one evening, one weekly) side by side.

name_lang.title: Instance name and language
name_lang.description: Give this instance a name and pick the language the AI should reply in.

logical_day_strategy.title: Logical day — strategy
logical_day_strategy.description: Decide which calendar date the brief is about. The default "fixed cutoff at 4 AM" works for most setups. "Sleep sensor" uses a binary_sensor to detect wake. "Manual" requires a service call.

logical_day_fixed_cutoff.title: Logical day — fixed cutoff
logical_day_fixed_cutoff.description: The hour at which the brief considers the calendar day has advanced.

logical_day_sleep_sensor.title: Logical day — sleep sensor
logical_day_sleep_sensor.description: The brief refers to the day that follows the most recent detected sleep session.

data_description.sleep_sensor_entity: binary_sensor.* that indicates whether you are sleeping. Typically your wearable (Amazfit, Fitbit, Withings, …).
data_description.awake_state: The value the sensor takes when you are AWAKE. Usually "off" (sleeping = on, awake = off) or "false". Check the current state under Developer tools → States.
data_description.hard_fallback_hour: If no sleep session is detected within 36 h, the day rolls over at this hour (safety net).
data_description.lookback_hours: How far back to search for the most recent sleep session.
data_description.min_sleep_duration_minutes: Minimum length to count as a sleep session (filters out short naps).

trigger_level.title: Trigger — level
trigger_level.description: How should the brief decide when to fire?

trigger_schedule.title: Trigger — fixed schedule
trigger_schedule.description: Daily generation time and which weekdays are active.

trigger_sensor_based.title: Trigger — sensor-based
trigger_sensor_based.description: The brief fires once the sensor reaches a given state (typically "wake"), with a delay and an hour-of-day fallback.

ai_provider.title: AI — provider
ai_provider.description: Which AI writes the natural-language summary? "Disabled" produces a brief with empty AI sections. "HA ai_task" wraps an existing ai_task.* entity. Anthropic / OpenAI options use a direct API key.

ai_ha_ai_task.title: AI — ai_task entity
ai_ha_ai_task.description: Pick an ai_task.* entity already configured in Home Assistant.

ai_anthropic.title: AI — Anthropic (direct key)
ai_anthropic.description: Direct call to api.anthropic.com. The API key is stored on disk — prefer using !secret.

ai_openai.title: AI — OpenAI (direct key)
ai_openai.description: Direct call to api.openai.com. The API key is stored on disk — prefer using !secret.

copy_from.title: Copy fields from existing instance
copy_from.description: Optional: copy fields and categories from another morning_brief instance (one-shot, no live link).

error.unknown: Unknown error. Check the Home Assistant logs.

init.title: Morning Brief — options
init.description: Each section saves on submit and returns you here. To discard a section in progress, close the dialog with the X. Click "Done" to close cleanly.

general_basics.title: General — name + language
general_basics.description: Rename the instance and change the narration language. The AI provider lives in its own section.

ai_picker.title: AI — pick a provider
ai_picker.description: Which model writes the narration? "Disabled" produces a brief with empty AI sections. "HA ai_task" wraps an existing ai_task entity. Anthropic / OpenAI need a direct API key.

ai_ha_ai_task.title: AI — ai_task entity
ai_ha_ai_task.description: Pick an ai_task.* entity already configured in Home Assistant.

ai_anthropic.title: AI — Anthropic (direct key)
ai_anthropic.description: Direct call to api.anthropic.com. The API key is stored on disk — prefer using !secret.

ai_openai.title: AI — OpenAI (direct key)
ai_openai.description: Direct call to api.openai.com. The API key is stored on disk — prefer using !secret.

logical_day_picker.title: Logical day — strategy
logical_day_picker.description: Decide which calendar date the brief is about. "Fixed cutoff at 4 AM" works for most setups. "Sleep sensor" uses a binary_sensor to detect wake. "Manual" requires a service call.

logical_day_fixed_cutoff.title: Logical day — fixed cutoff
logical_day_fixed_cutoff.description: The hour at which the brief considers the calendar day has advanced.

logical_day_sleep_sensor.title: Logical day — sleep sensor
logical_day_sleep_sensor.description: The brief refers to the day that follows the most recent detected sleep session.

data_description.sleep_sensor_entity: binary_sensor.* that indicates whether you are sleeping. Typically your wearable (Amazfit, Fitbit, Withings, …).
data_description.awake_state: The value the sensor takes when you are AWAKE. Usually "off" (sleeping = on, awake = off) or "false". Check the current state under Developer tools → States.
data_description.hard_fallback_hour: If no sleep session is detected within 36 h, the day rolls over at this hour (safety net).
data_description.lookback_hours: How far back to search for the most recent sleep session.
data_description.min_sleep_duration_minutes: Minimum length to count as a sleep session (filters out short naps).

trigger_picker.title: Trigger — level
trigger_picker.description: How should the brief decide when to fire?

trigger_schedule.title: Trigger — fixed schedule
trigger_schedule.description: Daily generation time and which weekdays are active.

trigger_sensor_based.title: Trigger — sensor-based
trigger_sensor_based.description: The brief fires once the sensor reaches a given state, with a delay and an hour-of-day fallback.

notification.title: Notification
notification.description: Where to send the brief and how to make it tappable.

data_description.notify_service: Short name of the HA notify service (without the notify. prefix).
data_description.click_action_url: URL opened when the user taps the notification (e.g. your Lovelace dashboard).
data_description.notification_pinned_fields: Comma-separated list of field IDs to highlight in the notification body.

persistence.title: Persistence
persistence.description: How many briefs to keep in history (5–365).

reorder_fields.title: Reorder fields

reorder_categories.title: Reorder categories

advanced.title: Advanced
advanced.description: Low-level settings useful for debugging and AI customisation. To see the default prompt currently in use, go back to the menu and pick "View default AI prompt".

data_description.log_level: Verbosity of morning_brief logs (DEBUG = very verbose, ERROR = silent).
data_description.prompt_template_override: Raw Jinja2 that replaces the default prompt. Leave empty to use the prompt shipped with the integration.
data_description.user_custom_context: Free-text injected into the prompt as "user context". Useful to set tone, flag day-of constraints, etc.
data_description.expose_preview_service: When enabled, morning_brief.preview can be called to generate a brief without persisting it (useful for testing a new prompt).

view_default_prompt.title: Default AI prompt ({report_type})
view_default_prompt.description:
Here is the Jinja2 prompt currently used to generate the brief. To customise it, copy this, edit it, then paste it into Advanced → AI prompt template override.

```
{prompt}
```

Submit to return to the menu.

pool_fields.title: Shared fields
pool_fields.description:
Fields currently in the shared pool:

{listing}

To CREATE a new field, use the "+ Add sub-item" button on the integration page (the create flow writes to this pool too).
From here you can edit a field's VISIBILITY (which instances it applies to) or DELETE it from the pool.

pool_categories.title: Shared categories
pool_categories.description:
Categories currently in the shared pool:

{listing}

To CREATE a new category, use the "+ Add sub-item" button on the integration page.
From here you can edit the VISIBILITY or DELETE.

pool_edit_applicable.title: Visibility — {label} ({kind})
pool_edit_applicable.description: Pick the brief instances this item should appear in. Leave empty = visible in EVERY instance.

user.title: Field — entity
user.description: Pick the HA sensor that backs this field. The next step will propose the provider types that match this sensor, with a recommendation.

provider_pick.title: Field — provider type
provider_pick.description:
{recommendation}

Pick the type that matches your sensor's behaviour. Only types compatible with the chosen sensor are shown.

applicable_to.title: Field — applicable instances
applicable_to.description: Select the briefs this field should appear in. Leaving empty means visible in EVERY brief (recommended for shared fields).

applicable_to.title: Category — applicable instances
applicable_to.description: Select the briefs this category should appear in. Leaving empty means visible in EVERY brief.

generate.name: Generate brief
generate.description: Generate a brief for the selected instance.

preview.name: Preview brief
preview.description: Generate a brief without persisting or notifying.

advance_day.name: Advance logical day
advance_day.description: For the manual logical-day strategy: advance the logical date by one day.

clear_history.name: Clear history
clear_history.description: Delete all stored briefs for this instance.

test_ai_provider.name: Test AI provider
test_ai_provider.description: Validate the configured AI provider credentials.

get_last_brief.name: Get last brief
get_last_brief.description: Return the most recent brief's canonical JSON.

reorder_fields.name: Reorder fields
reorder_fields.description: Set the display order for fields.

options.ha_ai_task: HA ai_task (wraps an existing ai_task entity)

options.cumulative: Daily counter — sensor that resets to 0 each day (steps, kWh consumed, sleep minutes)
options.instantaneous: Instantaneous value — read at the moment (temperature, heart rate, weight)
options.event_based: Point-in-time reading — sensor that only updates on an event (Wi-Fi scale, manual reading)
options.state: Non-numeric state — sensor that takes discrete values (Tempo colour, presence, status)
options.duration: Elapsed time — duration since a past event (litter maintenance, last visit, last clean)
options.calendar: Calendar — upcoming events from a calendar.* entity
options.weather: Weather — composite block (weather.* entity or Open-Meteo sensor)
options.manual: Manual — value entered via input_number / input_text / input_datetime

options.input_datetime: input_datetime — the entity IS the reference timestamp (recommended)
options.sensor_last_changed: last_changed — use the sensor's last_changed metadata
options.sensor_attribute_datetime: Attribute datetime — read a datetime from a sensor attribute

options.same_week_last_year: Same week last year (needs ≥53 weeks of LTS)
